key_risks: Hot June CPI (Jul 14) or hawkish Fed repricing could hit high-duration growth and semis hardest.
Semiconductor momentum mean-reverts after a 21% 30-day run, dragging the concentrated tech tilt.
Soft June payrolls (Jul 2) or weaker retail sales (Jul 16) could undercut cyclical industrials exposure.
Gold drawdown if real yields rise further on hawkish dots.

portfolio.rationale: Broad large-cap core with strong trend; benchmark-relative risk controlled while capturing continued melt-up.
Strongest momentum leader; AI capex cycle and chip demand driving outsized returns, with continuation likely into July earnings.
Mega-cap growth strength confirmed by recent breadth and Nasdaq leadership into the window.
Solid Philly Fed orders, resilient economy and defense/aerospace tailwinds; cyclically strong without crowding into a single theme.
Hedge against sticky inflation (CPI 4.2% YoY, hawkish dots) and any risk-off shock during the window.

portfolio_rationale: Macro shows resilient growth, strong retail sales and firm tech/semis momentum, favoring a continuation tilt toward large-cap growth and semis while anchoring in the S&P 500 benchmark with a small gold hedge against sticky inflation.
rationale_summary: Tilt toward continuation in tech/semis and broad large-caps given strong momentum and solid macro, with modest diversification into industrials and gold.
